blurb: Manages Alberta's recovery-oriented system of mental health and addiction care — providing crisis support, counselling, addiction treatment, recovery communities, harm reduction services, and child and youth mental health programs.

goals.title: OUTCOME 1: Albertans can access mental health support and crisis services
goals.description: The ministry ensures Albertans can access mental health services including crisis intervention, counselling, and community-based support for adults, children, and youth.

foundational_services.name: Plan and fund the recovery-oriented system of mental health care
foundational_services.desc: Develops policy and funding models for Alberta's recovery-oriented mental health system, including commissioning services through Recovery Alberta, licensing service providers, and measuring system performance outcomes.

main_services.name: Get mental health crisis support or find counselling

supporting.name: Get crisis support for a mental health or addiction emergency
supporting.desc: Albertans in mental health or addiction crisis can access immediate help through 211 Alberta (24/7, over 250 languages), Kids Help Phone (ages 11–25), and emergency services. Crisis support includes phone, text, and online chat.
supporting.users: Albertans in crisis, family members, bystanders

reach_estimate.estimate: Tens of thousands of crisis contacts annually
reach_estimate.basis: 24/7 crisis lines serving all Albertans in over 250 languages

economic_impact.note: Crisis intervention prevents hospitalization, self-harm, and reduces emergency department demand

supporting.name: Find affordable counselling for mental health or substance use
supporting.desc: Counselling Alberta provides affordable, accessible in-person and online counselling services with no waitlist. Sliding-scale fees ensure services are available regardless of income. Recovery Alberta also provides community-based counselling.
supporting.users: Adults, youth, and families seeking mental health or substance use counselling

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of thousands of Albertans access mental health counselling annually
reach_estimate.basis: Growing demand for accessible mental health services across the province

economic_impact.note: Early counselling intervention prevents escalation to crisis and reduces long-term system costs

supporting.name: Find community mental health services near you
supporting.desc: Recovery Alberta and community partners deliver outpatient mental health services across the province including assessment, therapy, day programs, and psychiatric consultation. Services are available in person and virtually.
supporting.users: Albertans with mental health concerns, family members

reach_estimate.estimate: Tens of thousands of clients served through community programs
reach_estimate.basis: Province-wide network of outpatient mental health services

economic_impact.note: Community-based care keeps people in their communities and reduces hospital admissions

main_services.name: Access mental health support for children and youth

supporting.name: Get mental health support for a child or youth
supporting.desc: Kickstand provides free, confidential mental health and addiction services for ages 11–25, including peer support, Indigenous wellness, and social services. CASA Mental Health offers school-based therapy and residential programs for children and youth.

reach_estimate.estimate: Thousands of children and youth served annually
reach_estimate.basis: Growing demand for youth mental health services across the province

economic_impact.note: Early intervention in youth mental health prevents lifelong chronic conditions and system dependency

supporting.name: Find resources for supporting a young person with mental health challenges
supporting.desc: Parents, caregivers, and educators can access resources, information, and support for helping children and youth manage mental health and substance use challenges, including bullying support and crisis safety planning.

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of thousands of parents and educators
reach_estimate.basis: Universal concern among families with children and youth

economic_impact.note: Empowered caregivers improve early identification and reduce crisis escalation

goals.title: OUTCOME 2: Albertans can access addiction treatment and recovery services
goals.description: The ministry provides a continuum of addiction services from harm reduction and crisis intervention through treatment and long-term recovery, delivered through Recovery Alberta and community partners.

foundational_services.name: Commission and fund addiction treatment and recovery services
foundational_services.desc: Funds and oversees the continuum of addiction services including detoxification, opioid agonist treatment, residential treatment, recovery communities, and community-based outpatient programs through Recovery Alberta and contracted providers.

main_services.name: Get treatment for addiction or substance use

supporting.name: Access addiction treatment or detoxification services
supporting.desc: Recovery Alberta provides free detoxification and addiction treatment through community-based outpatient programs, day programs, and residential facilities. Treatment options include medically supported withdrawal management, counselling, and opioid agonist treatment.
supporting.users: Albertans with substance use disorders, family members

reach_estimate.estimate: Tens of thousands of Albertans access treatment annually
reach_estimate.basis: Significant and growing demand for addiction services province-wide

economic_impact.note: Addiction treatment reduces emergency visits, crime, and social costs while restoring productive lives

supporting.name: Access same-day virtual addiction medicine through the opioid dependency program
supporting.desc: The Virtual Opioid Dependency Program (VODP) provides same-day access to addiction medicine specialists by phone for Albertans with opioid use disorder. Available 8am–8pm daily, no referral required.

reach_estimate.basis: Targeted program for opioid use disorder with no-referral access

economic_impact.note: Same-day access to opioid treatment reduces overdose risk and emergency department burden

supporting.name: Apply to a recovery community for long-term residential treatment
supporting.desc: Alberta's recovery communities provide up to one year of free, live-in treatment including opioid agonist medication, counselling, employment training, financial and housing support, and peer mentorship. Currently four communities are operational, with more planned including five in partnership with Indigenous nations.

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of residential beds across 4+ communities
reach_estimate.basis: New program expanding to 11 communities including Indigenous-led sites

economic_impact.note: Long-term residential recovery produces sustained outcomes and reduces chronic service use

main_services.name: Access harm reduction and overdose prevention services

supporting.name: Get a free naloxone kit
supporting.desc: Free naloxone kits are available at over 2,000 locations across Alberta including pharmacies, health centres, and community organizations. Naloxone reverses the effects of an opioid overdose and can save lives.
supporting.users: People who use opioids, friends, family members, community members

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of thousands of kits distributed annually

economic_impact.note: Naloxone directly prevents overdose deaths — the most immediate life-saving intervention available

supporting.name: Access supervised drug consumption or overdose prevention services
supporting.desc: Supervised drug consumption services operate in Calgary, Edmonton, Grande Prairie, and Lethbridge, providing a safe space to use substances under medical supervision. The Digital Overdose Response System (DORS) app is also available for solo users.
supporting.users: People who use drugs, community members

reach_estimate.estimate: Tens of thousands of visits to supervised consumption sites annually
reach_estimate.basis: Four sites operating across major Alberta centres

economic_impact.note: Supervised consumption prevents overdose deaths and connects users with treatment pathways

supporting.name: Learn about substance risks and drug safety information
supporting.desc: The ministry provides evidence-based information about the risks of alcohol, cannabis, opioids, methamphetamine, and other substances through the DrugSafe program and online resources, supporting informed decisions about substance use.
supporting.users: All Albertans, parents, educators, health providers

channels.label: Drug safety publications and fact sheets

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of thousands of page views and resource downloads
reach_estimate.basis: Broad public interest in substance use information, especially among parents

economic_impact.note: Evidence-based information reduces risky substance use and supports earlier help-seeking

goals.title: OUTCOME 3: Mental health and addiction services are safe and accountable
goals.description: The ministry licenses and regulates addiction and mental health service providers, ensures service quality, and supports evidence-based system improvement through the Canadian Centre of Recovery Excellence.

foundational_services.name: License and regulate addiction and mental health service providers
foundational_services.desc: Licenses addiction treatment facilities and mental health service providers to ensure services meet safety, quality, and evidence-based standards. Conducts inspections and enforces compliance.

main_services.name: Find a licensed treatment provider or report a concern

supporting.name: Find a licensed addiction or mental health treatment provider
supporting.desc: Albertans can search for licensed addiction and mental health service providers across the province, including residential treatment facilities, outpatient programs, and recovery services.
supporting.users: Albertans seeking treatment, family members, referral agencies

reach_estimate.estimate: Tens of thousands of searches annually
reach_estimate.basis: Albertans and families searching for quality-assured treatment options

economic_impact.note: Licensing gives Albertans confidence that providers meet safety and quality standards

relationships.service: License and regulate addiction and mental health service providers

supporting.name: Report a concern about a mental health or addiction service provider
supporting.desc: Albertans can report safety, quality, or conduct concerns about licensed addiction and mental health service providers to the ministry for investigation.

reach_estimate.basis: Regulatory oversight of licensed treatment facilities

economic_impact.note: Accountability protects vulnerable clients and maintains public trust in the treatment system

relationships.service: License and regulate addiction and mental health service providers
